rule_text: All items must be written in third person. Avoid use of 'I', 'us', 'our', 'we', 'my'. Replace with the organization name or neutral phrasing.
All submissions must be concise and clear. This is not the space to be witty or poetic.
Avoid exclamation marks in headlines and minimize them in body text. Use professional, neutral tone.
All event-related listings must include the time, date, and location of the event. If an activity is not free, a price must be included.
Use 'Today' and 'Tomorrow' instead of the specific date when appropriate. This applies to dates in both headlines and body text.
Embed hyperlinks in meaningful anchor text rather than displaying raw URLs. Use descriptive link text like 'Learn more', 'Register', 'Apply now'.
Use AP style for dates: abbreviate months with 6+ letters (Jan., Feb., Aug., Sept., Oct., Nov., Dec.). Spell out March, April, May, June, July. Use numerals for dates (Jan. 5, not January fifth).
Use AP style for times: lowercase a.m. and p.m. with periods. Use noon and midnight instead of 12 p.m. and 12 a.m. Use figures: 1 p.m., 3:30 p.m. Use 'to' for ranges: 9 a.m. to 5 p.m.
Lengthy submissions should display with a few sentences in the newsletter and a link to a website containing the entire message. UCM reserves the right to trim lengthy submissions.
Items generally run once. They can run twice if there is an RSVP or registration component.
Items running more than once should not be resubmitted with altered text unless the original has an error.
Announcements should be chronological, with more important items at the top (e.g., university-wide announcements, urgent notices).
All submissions must be affiliated with the University of Idaho.
Thesis defenses are not included in the newsletter.
Sports competitions outside the Moscow-Pullman, Lewiston-Clarkston area are not included.
Limited-audience application deadlines and items intended for a small subset of the audience should be handled through other means.
